diagrams.title: Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange Protocol Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step message exchange between Alice and Bob with mathematical computations at each stage
elements: Step 2: Alice computes A = g^a mod p, sends to Bob
Step 3: Bob computes B = g^b mod p, sends to Alice
Step 4: Alice computes S = B^a mod p
Step 5: Bob computes S = A^b mod p
Eve observes A and B but cannot compute S without solving DLP
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the sequential flow of DH protocol showing how shared secret is established

diagrams.title: Man-in-the-Middle Attack on Diffie-Hellman
diagrams.description: Attacker's position between Alice and Bob showing two separate key exchanges
elements: Alice thinks she exchanges keys with Bob but actually exchanges with Eve
Eve exchanges keys with Bob pretending to be Alice
Eve has two shared secrets: S_AE with Alice, S_BE with Bob
Eve can decrypt, re-encrypt, and forward all messages
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate vulnerability of unauthenticated DH to MITM attack

diagrams.title: Key Derivation from DH Shared Secret
diagrams.description: Process flow showing how DH secret is transformed into cryptographic keys for encryption
elements: DH Shared Secret g^(ab) mod p
Output: Encryption Key, MAC Key, IV/Nonce
PRNG seeded with derived keys for keystream generation
diagrams.purpose: Show connection between key exchange and PRNG for cryptographic operations
